ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for shokupan or black sesame filled milk bread. FOODON_00002683 (bread food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E (Bread and similar products) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for enriched Japanese-style milk bread with sesame paste filling.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226695000 (Bread) is the closest available parent concept. No specific code for shokupan or sesame-filled milk bread exists in SNOMED CT.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is approximately 120 g, representing one thick slice or a small individual loaf portion of this enriched milk bread.

nutrient_source.title: Bread, white, commercially prepared (USDA SR Legacy 18069) and Black sesame seeds, whole (USDA FoodData Central 170150) — composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for black sesame shokupan exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing: (1) USDA SR Legacy 18069 enriched white bread as the base dough matrix (approximately 75% by weight), and (2) USDA FoodData Central 170150 black sesame seeds/paste (approximately 25% by weight), adjusted for the enriched milk-bread formulation (added butter, condensed milk, cream) which increases fat, sugar, and energy relative to standard white bread. Values represent a best-estimate composite and should be verified against laboratory analysis or a manufacturer's nutrition panel when available.

gi_source.title: White bread GI reference — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for black sesame shokupan. GI of 70 is estimated from white bread (GI 70, glucose reference) as the closest analogous food per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. The enriched milk-bread dough matrix is predominantly refined wheat flour, which drives GI similarly to white bread. The black sesame paste filling contributes fat and fibre that may modestly lower the effective GI, but in the absence of direct measurement, GI 70 (high) is used as a conservative estimate. Glycemic load per 120 g serving = (70 × 48.0 × 1.20) / 100 = 40.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg/100 g) from enriched dough and condensed milk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit portion size to one thin slice (~60 g), contributing approximately 186 mg sodium, and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods in the same meal to stay within the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~70) and significant refined carbohydrate load (~48 g/100 g) with added sugars from condensed milk and sesame paste
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as an occasional food only; a standard 120 g serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 40, which is very high, and post-meal blood glucose monitoring is strongly advised if consumed.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~4.8 g/100 g) from butter, cream, and condensed milk, alongside moderate sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake due to the combined saturated fat and sodium load; choosing a smaller portion (~60 g) and pairing with unsaturated-fat-rich foods can help mitigate the cardiovascular impact within a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Intense Black Sesame Shokupan is a premium enriched Japanese-style milk bread (shokupan) filled with a rich, low-sugar black sesame paste, sold at RUXU (入续 糖水铺与包) in Singapore. The bread dough is made from wheat flour, whole milk, light cream, fine sugar, condensed milk, butter, and milk powder, producing an exceptionally soft, pillowy, and slightly sweet crumb characteristic of authentic shokupan. The filling is a dense, aromatic black sesame paste that delivers a deep, nutty, slightly bitter flavour profile balanced by the sweetness of the milk bread. Per 100 g, the product provides approximately 340 kcal, 7.8 g protein, 12.5 g fat (of which 4.8 g saturated), 48 g carbohydrate, and 10.5 g sugar. The glycemic index is estimated at 70 (high), making it a high-glycemic food unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. The sodium content (~310 mg/100 g) warrants attention for those managing hypertension. Black sesame seeds contribute meaningful amounts of calcium, iron, magnesium, and polyunsaturated fatty acids, partially offsetting the refined-carbohydrate profile. This product is vegetarian but not vegan (contains dairy), not certified halal, and contains gluten and sesame allergens. It is best consumed fresh on the day of purchase and is typically served as a breakfast item, afternoon snack, or gift pastry in the Singapore Chinese bakery context.
common_user_queries: How many calories in black sesame milk bread?
Can diabetics eat black sesame bread?
What is the GI of shokupan?
What allergens are in black sesame shokupan?
What is the difference between shokupan and regular bread?
How much sodium is in black sesame toast?

confidence_reason: No direct laboratory analysis or manufacturer nutrition panel was available for this specific product. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing USDA SR Legacy enriched white bread (18069) and USDA FoodData Central black sesame seeds (170150), adjusted for the enriched milk-bread formulation. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (white bread, GI 70) per the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium because the composite estimation approach introduces uncertainty, particularly for fat, sugar, and micronutrient values.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates based on USDA FoodData Central data for enriched white bread and black sesame seeds; they should be replaced with manufacturer-verified or laboratory-analysed values when available. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA FoodData Central source entries (SR Legacy 18069 and 170150) used for this composite estimation. 3. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; this is confirmed zero as neither enriched white bread nor black sesame paste contains meaningful vitamin C, and baking destroys any residual trace amounts — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central source data. 4. trans_fat_g of 0.1 reflects trace naturally occurring trans fats from the butter component, consistent with USDA data for butter-containing baked goods. 5. Halal status is listed as false because no halal certification information is available from the field survey listing; this should be verified directly with RUXU restaurant. 6. The GI of 70 is a conservative analogous estimate from white bread; the actual GI may be slightly lower due to the fat and fibre contribution of the black sesame paste filling. 7. Serving size of 120 g is an estimate for one thick slice or small individual loaf portion; actual product weight should be confirmed with the restaurant.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed composite nutrient estimate from USDA SR Legacy 18069 (enriched white bread) and USDA FoodData Central 170150 (black sesame seeds) as no direct source entry exists.
Estimated GI of 70 from white bread analogue per University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008.
Added Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and Japanese local names to satisfy G6 multilingual requirements for Singaporean Chinese cuisine.
Documented vitamin_c_mg = 0.0 as confirmed zero in notes as required by G4 zero-macro justification rule.
Used only allowed ontology match_type values (exact, closest_parent, none) per G5.
Verified all aliases_by_language entries appear verbatim in flat aliases list per Q8 consistency rule.
Set halal flag to false with caveat note as no certification information available.

Revised the documentation for vitamin_d_mcg = -1 in the 'notes' field to explicitly state the source databases where it is not reported, addressing the g3_numeric_hygiene hard gate failure from the previous iteration.
